stories.title: Starmer Resigns — Burnham Poised to Succeed as PM
stories.summary: Keir Starmer resigned on 22 June 2026 after a vote of no confidence, saying his successor would face 'a battle for the soul of the nation.' Andy Burnham is the clear frontrunner to become Labour leader and Prime Minister.

stories.title: Lucy Powell Speaks on Why Starmer Stepped Down
stories.summary: Deputy Labour leader Lucy Powell addressed the media on why Sir Keir Starmer decided to resign as Prime Minister, as the Labour leadership contest heats up.

stories.title: Burnham Faces Defence Spending Challenge at No. 10
stories.summary: A minister called on Andy Burnham, expected next PM, to show a credible path to meeting NATO's 3.5% of GDP defence spending target.

topics.name: Parliament
topics.description: House of Commons, House of Lords & Legislation

stories.title: Commons Agenda 6–10 July: E-Petitions and Defence Scrutiny
stories.summary: The House of Commons sits this week with Westminster Hall debates on e-petitions, and the Treasury Committee questioning Defence Ministers on the new Defence Investment Plan.

stories.title: Parliament Week 6–9 July: Outdoor Education Bill and School Regulation
stories.summary: This week includes a 10-Minute Rule Bill on Outdoor Education in the Commons and a Lords debate on school regulation following Galaxy Global academy closures.

topics.name: Labour Party
topics.description: Labour Party news, policy & internal politics

stories.title: 2026 Labour Leadership Election Underway — Seven Candidates Standing
stories.summary: Triggered by Starmer's 22 June resignation, the Labour leadership contest has attracted seven candidates. Andy Burnham is the frontrunner after his decisive Makerfield by-election win.

stories.title: Burnham's Fiscal Challenge: State of UK Finances Examined
stories.summary: Analysis examines the fiscal inheritance awaiting the likely next PM, including inherited debt, defence commitments, and uncosted manifesto pledges.

stories.title: Burnham's Rise Could Boost Liverpool Jobs via Devolution Push
stories.summary: Andy Burnham's advocacy for greater devolution and investment in the North is seen as a potential catalyst for economic development, including in his native Merseyside.

stories.title: Badenoch: Burnham Should Sack Miliband, Not Make Him Chancellor
stories.summary: Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch urged any incoming Labour PM to dismiss Ed Miliband, saying net zero policies are destroying UK energy infrastructure and competitiveness.

stories.title: Tories Mark Two Years of Labour Government
stories.summary: The Conservative Party marked two years since the 2024 general election result, highlighting what they describe as Labour's failures and positioning Badenoch as the alternative PM.

stories.title: Lib Dems Claim Credit for Extended Pub Hours During World Cup
stories.summary: The Liberal Democrats claimed they pressured PM Starmer into passing last-minute legislation extending pub closing times during England's World Cup matches, calling it a win for their campaigning.

stories.title: Ipsos Poll: Lib Dems Fall One Point as Reform Leads
stories.summary: A new Ipsos voting intention poll shows Reform on 26% and Labour on 24%, with the Liberal Democrats falling one percentage point as Reform's overall lead narrows by 4 points since May.

stories.title: Farage Referred to Parliamentary Standards Watchdog Over Undeclared Gifts
stories.summary: Nigel Farage has been referred to the parliamentary standards watchdog after reports he failed to declare benefits — including a reported £5m in support — from George Cottrell, a convicted fraudster and crypto entrepreneur. Farage denies wrongdoing.

stories.title: Could Farage Quit? Pressure Mounts as Restore Party Rises
stories.summary: Speculation is growing about Farage's future as Reform leader amid the Cottrell gifts scandal, by-election losses, and the emergence of rival party Restore. Reform allies say exit talk is 'wishful thinking'.

stories.title: Government Announces £100k Cap on Political Donations from Overseas Voters
stories.summary: Ministers announced a crackdown on large political donations as part of a review of political funding integrity, with overseas voters moving to the UK to face a £100,000 donations cap.

stories.title: Police Scotland Probe Yes Scotland's Missing £1.5m
stories.summary: Police Scotland are investigating a formal complaint alleging that £1.5 million raised during the 2014 Yes Scotland independence campaign is unaccounted for.

stories.title: Burnham Privately Rules Out Second Independence Referendum
stories.summary: Andy Burnham has reportedly told Scottish Labour MPs he will flatly reject indyref2 if he becomes Prime Minister, prompting a backlash from the SNP.

stories.title: SNP: Burnham's Devolution Claims 'Unravelling' Over IndyRef2 Stance
stories.summary: The SNP has accused Andy Burnham of contradicting his devolution agenda by opposing a second independence referendum, saying his claims are 'unravelling'.

stories.title: UK Tightens Rules on Overseas Political Donations
stories.summary: The UK government has tightened rules on overseas political donations to stop foreign money influencing elections, as part of a broader political funding review triggered by a former Reform MP jailed for bribery.

stories.title: Parliament Week: Treasury Committee to Question Defence Ministers
stories.summary: As part of the busy parliamentary week of 6–9 July, the Treasury Committee will question Defence Ministers on spending levels and the recently published Defence Investment Plan.

stories.title: Makerfield By-election: Burnham Wins, Opening Path to Labour Leadership
stories.summary: Andy Burnham won the Makerfield by-election on 18 June 2026 with a decisive majority, vacating his Greater Manchester mayoralty to contest the Labour leadership. Labour polled 38%, ahead of Restore (29%) and Reform (24%).

stories.title: New Ipsos Poll: Reform Leads at 26%, Labour Up 4 Points to 24%
stories.summary: The latest Ipsos headline voting intention shows Reform at 26% and Labour at 24%, with Reform's lead over Labour narrowing by 4 percentage points since May as the Labour leadership race energises the party.

stories.title: Railways Bill Debated in Lords This Week
stories.summary: The House of Lords is debating the Railways Bill during its 6–9 July sitting, with industry leaders calling for amendments to better support economic growth and private investment.

stories.title: Lords to Question Government on West Bank Settlement Land Purchases
stories.summary: Lords members will question the government this week on the purchase of settlement land on the West Bank by British citizens and the application of international law.

stories.title: Human Rights Due Diligence Bill Introduced in Lords
stories.summary: A mandatory human rights and environmental due diligence bill was introduced in the House of Lords in June, requiring companies to assess and address human rights risks in their supply chains.
